channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy will visit the White House Friday to sign US-Ukraine critical minerals deal
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Donald Trump says Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy will visit the White House on Friday to sign a critical minerals deal. Trump made the announcement Wednesday at start of his first Cabinet meeting. THIS IS A BREAKING NEWS UPDATE. AP’s earlier story follows below.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian prosecutors launch investigation into far-right populist who led canceled presidential vote
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanian prosecutors launched a criminal investigation on Wednesday against a far-right populist who won the first round in a canceled presidential election last year, accusing him of election campaign funding abuses, supporting fascist groups and other offenses.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN food agency pauses aid to famine-hit Sudan displacement camp of half a million people
item.description: The United Nations’ food agency says it has paused aid distribution in Sudan’s famine-hit Zamzam displacement camp of a half-million people as fighting intensifies between the country’s warring sides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A court in Bosnia sentences separatist Bosnian Serb President Milorad Dodik to 1 year in prison
item.description: A court in Bosnia has sentenced separatist Bosnian Serb President Milorad Dodik to one year in prison and banned him from politics for six years because of his unconstitutional actions.
item.dc:creator: Amer Cohadzic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: BP to slash spending on net zero ventures as it focuses on oil and gas again
item.description: British energy company BP has confirmed it is to slash spending on green ventures and up its oil and gas production.
item.dc:creator: Pan Pylas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israelis bid farewell to Shiri Bibas and her 2 young sons killed in captivity in Gaza
item.description: Tens of thousands of Israelis have lined highways holding flags, orange balloons and signs saying “forgive us” in an outpouring of grief as the bodies of a mother and her two small sons who were killed in captivity in the Gaza Strip were taken for burial.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Lidman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Analysts back Trump’s USAID cuts in Africa, say increased trade will better benefit continent's poor
item.description: Some analysts believe that President Donald Trump was right to cut aid to Africa and that increased trade with the continent will fill "the plates of hungry kids at the end of the day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Stress and fear roil a tiny, rapidly militarizing Japanese island near Taiwan
item.description: YONAGUNI, Japan (AP) — This tiny island on Japan's western frontier has no chain convenience stores. Nature lovers can dive with hammerhead sharks and watch miniature horses graze on a hill. But the wooded mountain ranges now carry radar sites.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel and Hamas agree on new exchange, leaving fragile ceasefire intact
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli and Hamas officials said Tuesday they have reached an agreement to exchange the bodies of dead hostages for the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, keeping their fragile ceasefire intact for at least a few more days.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least four dead in South Korea highway construction project collapse
item.description: At least four people were killed and six others were injured after concrete structures supporting a bridge collapsed at a highway construction site in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical but stable condition Tuesday as he worked from the hospital while battling double pneumonia, and the Vatican announced some major governing decisions that suggest he is getting essential work done and looking ahead.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO nation Poland scrambles air defenses as Russia strikes western Ukraine
item.description: Poland once again launched air defenses after Russia fired long-range strikes across Ukraine, hitting near the shared border with Poland just hours after leaders met in Kyiv to pledge more support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump refugee ban
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday blocked the Trump administration from suspending the U.S. refugee program, siding with resettlement groups that challenged a Day One order from the president. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Trial begins in Germany for 4 alleged Hamas members accused of seeking weapons caches across Europe
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A trial began Tuesday in Germany for four alleged Hamas members suspected of organizing weapons caches across Europe. The country's top prosecutor accuses the men of membership in a foreign terrorist organization. It’s a pilot case for prosecutors, German news agency dpa reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan coast guard detains Chinese-crewed vessel suspected of cutting undersea cable
item.description: Taiwan detained eight Chinese nationals crewing a ship suspected of severing an undersea cable connecting the island of Penghu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says Russia is open to economic cooperation with US on rare earth minerals
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said that Moscow and Washington could make a deal on mining rare earth minerals both in Russia and parts of Russian-occupied Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US votes against condemning Russia for Ukraine war as Trump admin chases peace deal
item.description: The U.S. voted against a U.N. resolution laying blame on Russia for its invasion of Ukraine and demanding Moscow remove its troops from Ukrainian territory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French surgeon accused of raping or abusing 299 people, mostly child patients
item.description: VANNES, France (AP) — A former surgeon went on trial in France on Monday for the alleged rape or sexual abuse of 299 victims, most of them children who were his patients, in what investigators and his own notebooks describe as a pattern of violence spanning over three decades.
item.dc:creator: JADE LE DELEY and NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korean hackers behind $1.5 billion crypto hack: Security firm
item.description: North Korean hackers were behind a sophisticated hack that stole about $1.5 billion worth of digital currency from Bybit, a major cryptocurrency exchange, according to security firm TRM.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: American tourist arrested in Paris after allegedly throwing newborn baby out of hotel window: reports
item.description: Paris authorities arrested an American woman on Monday after she allegedly killed her newborn by hurling the child out of a second-floor window, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Surviving Hamas: The story of Keith Siegel, an Israeli American hostage
item.description: An Israeli-American released from Hamas captivity weeks ago is doing 'OK,' his wife tells NewsNation's "Morning in America with Hena Doba.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia's invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MIKE PESOLI and STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: "We found that more than 5 billion people—ie, more than 60% of the world's population—do not have access to safe, quality, and affordable medical oxygen services," the commission wrote.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation
